caveats: The user may have run verification outside indexed agent sessions.

evidence_refs.label: No fresh command/test/browser proof found in compact history

open_questions: What is the smallest relevant command or browser task for this diff?
suggested_checks: Attach a fresh command, test, log, screenshot, or trace before marking the finding fixed.
evidence_candidates.why_it_matters: A review without fresh verification evidence can only prove static plausibility, not that the change works.

evidence_procedure_steps.action: Choose the smallest relevant repo command or browser task, rerun it, and attach the fresh output.
evidence_procedure_steps.artifact: command log, test report, screenshot, trace, or QA run artifact
evidence_procedure_steps.gate: No candidate is marked confirmed or fixed using stale or missing verification.
evidence_procedure_steps.input: Failed, stale, or missing command/test/browser evidence.
evidence_procedure_steps.output: Fresh pass/fail evidence linked to the review.

findings.suggestion: Replace with a slow, salted password KDF: `hashlib.scrypt`/`hashlib.pbkdf2_hmac` with a random per-user salt, or a dedicated library (bcrypt/argon2). Store salt+params alongside the hash. Update `verify_password` to read the stored salt/params and recompute accordingly.
findings.summary: source.py:8 `hashlib.md5(password.encode()).hexdigest()` hashes passwords with unsalted MD5. MD5 is fast and cryptographically broken; without a per-user salt, identical passwords yield identical digests, making rainbow-table and brute-force attacks trivial. The agent's own comment at source.py:6-7 acknowledges this exact defect but the code ships it anyway — a documented-but-unfixed bug (fake-green handoff risk).
findings.title: Unsalted MD5 used for password hashing

findings.suggestion: Add a test asserting round-trip (`verify_password(pw, hash_password(pw)) is True`), rejection of wrong passwords, that two hashes of the same password differ (salt present), and run it: `python -m pytest` (or `python -c` round-trip). Attach the output before marking fixed.
findings.summary: Both `hash_password` and `verify_password` are new with 0 callers and no accompanying tests; the pre-review search flagged `no-fresh-verification-evidence` and the `rerun_relevant_verification` gate is still blocked. Because the correct fix changes the stored-hash format (salt+params vs bare MD5 hex), any test must be authored against the replacement KDF, not the current MD5 output.
findings.title: No tests or verification for new auth primitives (handoff gap)

findings.summary: verify_password (source.py:12) compares with `hash_password(password) == stored`, which short-circuits and is not timing-safe. Secondary to the MD5 weakness, but if the hashing is fixed this comparison remains a (minor) timing side-channel.
findings.title: Non-constant-time hash comparison in verify_password

nodes.label: Prior commits, decisions, agents, and command evidence

nodes.label: rerun_relevant_verification

summary:
Risk-tiered review (lite) completed.

New file source.py implements password hashing with unsalted MD5. The self-labeled `# BUG:` comment is honest and matches the code, so there is no assumption/intent contradiction to flag — the issue is the insecure primitive itself, a real (high→critical-if-wired) security hole. Currently 0 callers (blast-radius confirmed), so no persisted-data or caller-contract breakage exists yet. Secondary: non-constant-time comparison in verify_password.

A 12-line new file implementing password hashing on unsalted MD5. This is a critical credential-storage defect: MD5 is broken and unsalted, making stored passwords trivially recoverable on any DB leak (source.py:8), with verify_password (source.py:12) also using non-constant-time == comparison. Currently 0 callers, so no live user flow is affected yet, but the functions are clearly intended for an auth path — fix before adoption. No user-facing screens/states exist in this diff to evaluate for the product-safety scope; the single finding is an obvious critical cross-scope defect.

Small 12-line new module implementing password hashing with unsalted MD5 — a critical security defect that the agent explicitly documented in a `# BUG:` comment (source.py:6-8) but did not fix. This is a textbook agent-handoff failure mode: the code reads as intentional yet leaves a known vulnerability live, and a summary could easily report it as 'added password hashing / done'. Secondary issue: non-constant-time comparison in verify_password (source.py:12). No tests and no fresh verification evidence exist; the verification gate is blocked. Blast radius is currently 0 callers, so no regression to existing code, but this must be fixed before anything calls it.
